_doc.hook_md_contract: T1171: Each hook script ('.py') has a companion '.md' self-description file at the same path (e.g. '.agent/hooks/g-hk-pre-tool-call.md' for '.agent/hooks/g-hk-pre-tool-call.py'). The hook.md uses the 5-section template (# Hook / ## Fires On / ## What It Does / ## Side Effects / ## Related Tasks) defined by '/g-hook-create'. The runtime contract is: when a hook fires under BeforeTool (or any other event), the harness SHOULD read the matching hook.md and inject its content as additional_context so the agent knows what the hook just did or why a tool call was blocked. See 'skills/g-skl-platform-gemini/PLATFORM_SPEC.md' '## Hook System'.
_doc.hook_md_authoring: Use '/g-hook-create <hook-name> <event>' to scaffold a new pair atomically. See 'commands/g-hook-create.md'.
_doc.hook_md_source_of_truth: Source the human-facing hook description from the 'hook.md' file; this 'hooks.json' is for wiring only.
_doc.event_names: Gemini CLI official event names (11, verified against https://geminicli.com/docs/hooks/): SessionStart, SessionEnd, BeforeAgent, AfterAgent, BeforeModel, AfterModel, BeforeToolSelection, BeforeTool, AfterTool, PreCompress, Notification. Tool-event matchers are regex, lifecycle matchers exact, '*' or '' matches all, timeout in ms. T1626 (WS-A-3): this file previously carried Claude-format event keys and script paths under the Claude dot-dir prefix — a hard break, since that directory does not ship in the gemini repo. Event keys were translated to gemini's native casing (inverse of g_hk_core.PLATFORM_EVENT_MAP['gemini']) and every command and _hook_md reference re-pointed at '.agent/hooks/'.
_doc.gald3r_lifecycle_events: T1055/T1624: In addition to the harness-native events wired above, gald3r defines two gald3r-internal lifecycle events: 'pre_skill' and 'post_skill'. No harness exposes a native skill-boundary event, so these are dispatched by the gald3r skill/command runner (or fired manually) and are NOT auto-wired here. The former 'pre_session'/'post_session' internal event names were RETIRED by T1624 (WS-A-1, decision D-8): the session-trace hooks now fire on the CANONICAL session-start/stop events via g_hk_core.py CONCERN_CHAIN.
_doc.native_surface: T510/T1626: gemini's REAL native hook surface is the 'hooks' object in '.gemini/settings.json' (wired to the canonical-core entrypoints in '.gemini/hooks/', which dispatch via g_hk_core.py). This '.agent/hooks.json' wires the '.agent/' overlay's per-concern hooks in the same event schema; reconciling/removing the '.agent/' overlay is tracked separately (see T510 Status History) and remains out of scope here. T1628 (WS-A-5) disposition: the BeforeTool Claude/Cursor-era tool-name-union matchers are KEPT (harmless supersets; concern hooks self-gate on payload), the logging+vault chain hooks are wired under SessionStart/AfterAgent below (D-7 wire), the canonical 'g_hk_core.py' is fanned into '.agent/hooks/' so the g-hk-agent-complete INDIRECT_CONCERNS chat-logger launch resolves here too, and the wrkspc-manifest-check hook was deleted (zero consumers; superseded by g-skl-workspace VALIDATE). The '.gemini/settings.json' surface instead uses '*' matchers with payload self-gating concern hooks.
